MiniLang
Official language, IDE, and tooling docs
HomeGetting StartedLanguage ReferenceInterop
Serious language ecosystem

MiniLang is now documenting the real legacy runtime, not an imagined future shell.

The active platform combines the legacy MiniLang runtime, Monaco-based MiniLang Studio, reusable workspace libraries, a safe Windows interop bridge, new collection features like arrays and foreach, and an official docs site built around topic-accurate examples with expected results.

Language

Legacy MiniLang runtime with enums, arrays, foreach, indexing, and reusable functions.

Studio

WinUI host, Monaco editor, startup files, diagnostics, Release console runs, and structured inspectors.

Interop

Safe user-mode process, time, console, IO, and user helpers exposed through the `win` bridge.

Reusable Workspace

Core, Console, IO, Window, and Collections libraries plus runnable startup apps under a real folder layout.

Open the docs

Getting StartedLearnVariables and makeLearnFunctionsLearnArraysReferenceforeachReferenceOperators and expressionsReference
Documentation map
Getting Started

Set up the legacy MiniLang runtime, open MiniLang Studio, and run your first real `.mini.c` program.

Variables and make

Use `make` to declare mutable variables and assign numbers, strings, arrays, and reusable function results.

Functions

Declare functions with `fn`, explicit return types, typed parameters, and `give` return values.

Arrays

MiniLang arrays are mutable, zero-based, dynamic collections that work with literals, indexing, and builtin helpers.

foreach

Use legacy-style `foreach item in values:` loops to iterate through array values without manual indexing.

Operators and expressions

Numbers, strings, comparisons, function calls, interop calls, and array expressions all participate in MiniLang expressions.

Enums

Enums provide named symbolic values with member access like `Tone.Warm` and equality comparisons.

Structs

Structs group related fields and constructor-style initialization behind MiniLang's existing object model.

use and reusable libraries

Import reusable `.mini.c` files with `use` and organize shared code under `MiniLangLibraries`.

Windows interop

Use the safe `win` bridge to call approved user-mode Windows helpers from MiniLang code.

CLI

The legacy MiniLang CLI checks, runs, and inspects `.mini.c` files from the command line.

MiniLang Studio

MiniLang Studio is the WinUI host plus Monaco-based IDE shell for editing, diagnostics, startup files, and build/run workflows.

Diagnostics

MiniLang diagnostics explain syntax, scope, interop, and collection mistakes in both the CLI and Studio.

Compiler internals

How the active legacy MiniLang pipeline tokenizes, groups, validates, interprets, and executes source files today.
